deep_dive.title: TGV-KV: Text-Grounded KV Eviction for Vision-Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) inherit the auto-regressive generation paradigm and cache the keys and values (KV) of all previous tokens to accelerate inference, resulting in memory consumption that scales linearly with context length. This issue is particularly pronounced in VLMs due to substantial redundancy in the visual modality. Although KV cache eviction approaches can effectively reduce inference memory, they often incur significant performance degradation in VLMs, as most are designed for language models and overlook the inherent gap between text and vision. By systematically analyzing the modality gap in VLMs in this work, we argue that the importance of visual information should be grounded in textual guidance and accordingly propose a Text-Grounded KV Eviction method for VLMs (TGV-KV). TGV-KV comprises three submodules: (1) Text-Vision Budgeting (TVB) assigns budget to each layer based on the mutual information interaction. (2) Text-Weighted Ranking (TWR) assesses the priority of text and ranks vision importance based on weighted text-image attention. (3) Text-Prioritised Retention (TPR) policy strategically preserves text KV to avoid acute information loss. We evaluate TGV-KV across five models with different sizes and architectures, showing that TGV-KV preserves 99.2% full-KV accuracy on the VizWiz-VQA task with LLaVA-NeXT and boosts end-to-end throughput by 52.6% with an extreme retention budget of 5%. Code is available at https://github.com/Danielement321/TGV-KV.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: text-guided KV eviction for vision-language models to reduce memory and accelerate inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 文本引导的视觉语言模型KV缓存驱逐，减少内存并加速推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 文本引导的视觉语言模型KV缓存驱逐，减少内存并加速推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 文本引导的视觉语言模型KV缓存驱逐，减少内存并加速推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: TGV-KV proposes text-guided KV cache eviction for VLMs, leveraging textual guidance to selectively retain important visual keys/values, reducing memory consumption with minimal performance loss.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 视觉语言模型（VLM）推理时缓存所有token的键值对，内存随上下文线性增长，视觉模态冗余尤其严重。现有KV驱逐方法忽视文本-视觉模态差异，导致性能下降。本文提出文本引导的KV驱逐（TGV-KV），依据文本重要性对视觉信息进行排序和驱逐。实验表明，在保持高任务精度的同时，显著降低显存占用并加速推理。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 视觉语言模型（VLM）推理时缓存所有token的键值对，内存随上下文线性增长，视觉模态冗余尤其严重。现有KV驱逐方法忽视文本-视觉模态差异，导致性能下降。本文提出文本引导的KV驱逐（TGV-KV），依据文本重要性对视觉信息进行排序和驱逐。实验表明，在保持高任务精度的同时，显著降低显存占用并加速推理。

deep_dive.title: MomentKV: Closing the Directional Gap in KV Cache Eviction for Long-Context Inference
deep_dive.abstract: Autoregressive decoding in Transformer-based language models relies on the KV cache, whose memory footprint grows linearly with sequence length and becomes the primary bottleneck for long-context inference. KV cache eviction addresses this by retaining a fixed-size subset of key-value pairs and discarding the rest. We identify that a primary source of output degradation is not the residual attention mass on evicted tokens, which existing methods already minimize, but a directional mismatch between the retained and evicted token sets. Specifically, the evicted tokens in practice are often near-orthogonal to the retained ones. Thus, even a small evicted mass could have an oversized impact on the resulting direction distribution and amplify into substantial output error. This reveals a fundamental limit in existing strategies. To address this, we propose MomentKV, which maintains compact, small-size moment statistics over the evicted token set, including a count, key mean, value mean, and value-key covariance. During eviction, the moment statistics is leveraged to identify tokens already well aligned with and captured by the accumulated summary, keeping the evicted set geometrically regular. During inference, they yield a closed-form first-order approximation of the evicted attention output, forming a mutually reinforcing loop between selective eviction and accurate correction. On LongBench and RULER with LLaMA-3.1-8B-Instruct and Qwen3-4B-Instruct, MomentKV outperforms all baselines at every cache budget, with the largest gains under aggressive compression.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache eviction for long-context LLM inference, addressing directional mismatch
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对长上下文LLM推理的KV缓存驱逐，解决方向不匹配问题
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对长上下文LLM推理的KV缓存驱逐，解决方向不匹配问题
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对长上下文LLM推理的KV缓存驱逐，解决方向不匹配问题
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MomentKV identifies that KV cache eviction suffers from directional mismatch between retained and evicted tokens, and proposes a method to minimize this gap for better long-context inference.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 长上下文LLM推理中，KV缓存随序列长度线性增长，成为主要瓶颈。现有驱逐方法最小化被驱逐token的残差注意力质量，但忽略了保留集与驱逐集之间的方向不匹配。本文发现驱逐token往往与保留token近似正交，导致方向分布偏斜。MomentKV通过优化保留集的定向分布来减少这种不匹配，在长上下文任务上提升了输出质量。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 长上下文LLM推理中，KV缓存随序列长度线性增长，成为主要瓶颈。现有驱逐方法最小化被驱逐token的残差注意力质量，但忽略了保留集与驱逐集之间的方向不匹配。本文发现驱逐token往往与保留token近似正交，导致方向分布偏斜。MomentKV通过优化保留集的定向分布来减少这种不匹配，在长上下文任务上提升了输出质量。

deep_dive.title: Multi-Segment Attention: Enabling Efficient KV-Cache Management for Faster Large Language Model Serving
deep_dive.abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) inference relies on key-value (KV) caches to avoid redundant attention computation. While approximate KV cache retention techniques reduce memory usage by sacrificing model accuracy, lossless approaches instead evict KV cache blocks from GPU memory and reconstruct them on demand to preserve exact outputs. Existing lossless KV cache management systems primarily base eviction decisions on access frequency or positional heuristics, without considering how different KV cache blocks affect the execution efficiency of GPU attention kernels.   In this paper, we propose AsymCache, a computation-latency-aware KV cache management system for LLM inference that explicitly aligns cache residency decisions with GPU attention kernel performance, including three key components: Multi-Segment Attention (MSA) for efficient non-contiguous KV context processing, a cache eviction policy that jointly optimizes hit rate and position-aware recomputation cost, and an adaptive chunking scheduler for high hardware utilization. Experiments show that AsymCache reduces TTFT by up to 1.90-2.03x and time-per-output-token (TPOT) by 1.62-1.71x over latest baselines, confirming the effectiveness of the method in common workloads and validating its design goal of balancing computational efficiency with cache hit rate. Moreover, the low-level design of AsymCache allows seamless integration into agent serving systems such as Continuum, where it further reduces average job latency by up to 18.1%.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV-cache management for faster LLM serving
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: AsymCache manages KV caches with computation-latency awareness, improving LLM serving throughput and latency without sacrificing accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大型语言模型推理依赖KV缓存来避免重复计算，现有无损失缓存管理主要基于访问频率或位置启发式，未考虑不同缓存块对GPU注意力核执行效率的影响。本文提出AsymCache，一种计算延迟感知的KV缓存管理系统，通过显式对齐缓存驻留决策与注意力核效率，在不牺牲准确率的前提下加速LLM推理服务。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 大型语言模型推理依赖KV缓存来避免重复计算，现有无损失缓存管理主要基于访问频率或位置启发式，未考虑不同缓存块对GPU注意力核执行效率的影响。本文提出AsymCache，一种计算延迟感知的KV缓存管理系统，通过显式对齐缓存驻留决策与注意力核效率，在不牺牲准确率的前提下加速LLM推理服务。

deep_dive.title: Depth-Attention: Cross-Layer Value Mixing for Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Self-attention selects information freely across the sequence, but across depth, Transformers merely add each layer's output to the residual stream, so later layers cannot selectively reuse earlier-layer representations. Recent cross-layer methods improve this flow but operate on hidden states outside attention, adding state beyond the key-value cache at inference--a cost that becomes increasingly salient as modern LLMs compress the cache with grouped-query and multi-head latent attention. We introduce Depth-Attention, which performs this selection inside the attention module itself: before a layer attends over the sequence, its query attends over the keys of earlier layers at the same token position and mixes their values into the value that self-attention then reads. Because Depth-Attention reuses the standard attention queries, keys, and value-cache slots, storing depth-mixed values in place of the original values, it adds no parameters and introduces no persistent inference state beyond the standard key-value cache--the same cache size as a vanilla decoder and less than hidden-state-based cross-layer methods. On Qwen3-style decoders at 1.5B and 3B parameters, Depth-Attention attains the lowest perplexity and the highest average downstream accuracy, improving over the vanilla Transformer by up to 2.3 accuracy points and surpassing strong cross-layer baselines in perplexity and average accuracy, while adding under 0.01% extra arithmetic FLOPs and no additional persistent inference state. The gains hold from 360M to 3B parameters and extend to looped Transformers.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Cross-layer value mixing in attention to improve LLM inference efficiency, related to KV cache optimization
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 在注意力机制中进行跨层值混合以提升大语言模型推理效率，与 KV 缓存优化相关
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 在注意力机制中进行跨层值混合以提升大语言模型推理效率，与 KV 缓存优化相关
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 在注意力机制中进行跨层值混合以提升大语言模型推理效率，与 KV 缓存优化相关
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Depth-Attention, which lets each layer attend over earlier layers' keys at the same token to mix values, improving cross-layer information flow without extra KV cache overhead.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 自注意力在序列维度上自由选择信息，但跨层信息利用不足。本文提出深度注意力机制，在每个 token 位置让当前层的查询关注前几层的键并混合其值，从而在不增加 KV 缓存负担的情况下实现选择性跨层值混合。该方法与 GQA、MLA 等现代 KV 缓存压缩技术兼容，有效提升大语言模型推理效率，并减少冗余计算。

deep_dive.title: AdaPLD: Adaptive Retrieval and Reuse for Efficient Model-Free Speculative Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates generation by verifying multiple drafted tokens in a single target-model forward pass, reducing sequential decoding iterations. Model-free variants avoid auxiliary draft models by reusing text and model states already available during generation, but their speedup depends on the reliability of the constructed drafts. We identify two limitations of existing reuse-based methods: lexically anchored retrieval has limited recall under surface-form variation, and deterministic span copying can be brittle when the retrieved context does not uniquely determine the continuation. We propose \emph{AdaPLD}, a training-free method that adaptively improves both retrieval and draft construction. AdaPLD preserves high-precision lexical reuse while using semantic similarity to recover additional reuse opportunities when lexical matching fails. It further constructs branched reuse hypotheses to account for continuation uncertainty, rather than relying on a single copied span. Across diverse benchmarks, AdaPLD reduces target-model forward passes and achieves up to $3.10\times$ decoding speedup.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: model-free speculative decoding with adaptive retrieval and reuse, efficient LLM inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 无模型投机解码，自适应检索与复用，高效LLM推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 无模型投机解码，自适应检索与复用，高效LLM推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 无模型投机解码，自适应检索与复用，高效LLM推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: AdaPLD improves model-free speculative decoding by adaptive retrieval and draft construction, achieving higher speedup without training.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 无模型投机解码通过复用已有文本和模型状态来加速推理，但现有方法存在检索召回率低和复制脆弱的问题。本文提出AdaPLD，训练无关地自适应改进检索和草稿构建，保留高精度同时提高草稿可靠性。在多个LLM基准上，AdaPLD实现了更高的加速比且不损失生成质量。

quick_skim.title: Cost-Aware Diffusion Draft Trees for Speculative Decoding
quick_skim.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates inference by having a lightweight drafter propose tokens verified in parallel by the target language model. Block diffusion drafters such as DFlash generate an entire draft block in one pass, yielding per-position marginals; DDTree uses these to build a candidate tree that maximizes expected acceptance length under a fixed node budget. We observe, however, that acceptance length is non-decreasing in budget: it always favors larger trees regardless of verification cost, offering no principled basis for budget selection. We introduce \textbf{CaDDTree} (Cost-aware Diffusion Draft Tree), a method that directly optimizes token throughput (expected tokens generated per unit time) by jointly selecting the tree structure and node budget. We model draft and verification latencies explicitly, show that the throughput objective decomposes into a per-round one-dimensional search over the budget, and prove that under a convex verification cost the throughput function is \emph{unimodal}, enabling an efficient greedy stopping rule. CaDDTree requires no offline budget search, adapting the budget each round from the current per-position distributions and verification cost. Experiments on Qwen3-4B and Qwen3-8B across eight benchmarks spanning reasoning, coding, and instruction-following tasks show that \caDDTree{} matches or surpasses DDTree with oracle budget selection on nearly all tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: cost-aware diffusion draft tree for speculative decoding throughput optimization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 成本感知扩散草稿树优化投机解码吞吐量
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 成本感知扩散草稿树优化投机解码吞吐量
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 成本感知扩散草稿树优化投机解码吞吐量
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces CaDDTree, a method that jointly optimizes tree structure and node budget to directly maximize token throughput in diffusion-based speculative decoding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对扩散草稿投机解码中树结构选择缺乏成本意识的问题，提出CaDDTree，通过联合优化树结构和节点预算直接最大化令牌吞吐量。该方法考虑验证开销，在多样本模型和任务上实现了更高的生成效率，属于LLM高效推理中投机解码的进阶研究。

quick_skim.title: Parallel Jacobi Decoding for Fast Autoregressive Image Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Autoregressive (AR) models have demonstrated remarkable performance in generating high-fidelity images. However, their inherently sequential next-token prediction leads to significantly slower inference. Recent studies have introduced Jacobi-style decoding to accelerate autoregressive image generation. Extending the draft sequence initially improves efficiency, yet the acceleration quickly saturates as error propagation in the one-dimensional sequence hinders convergence. Observing that images exhibit strong local spatial correlations, we propose Parallel Jacobi Decoding (PJD), a training-free decoding approach that expands draft tokens in the two-dimensional spatial domain to enable efficient spatially parallel refinement. PJD adjusts the attention mask to mitigate error accumulation and improve convergence stability. Extensive experiments on diverse datasets show that PJD achieves 4.8x-6.4x acceleration across multiple autoregressive image generation models while maintaining competitive generation quality.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Parallel Jacobi decoding for fast autoregressive image generation shares core idea with speculative decoding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 并行雅可比解码加速自回归图像生成，其核心思想与投机解码相似
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 并行雅可比解码加速自回归图像生成，其核心思想与投机解码相似
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 并行雅可比解码加速自回归图像生成，其核心思想与投机解码相似
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Parallel Jacobi Decoding (PJD) to accelerate autoregressive image generation by expanding draft tokens in 2D spatial domain, achieving training-free speedup with spatial parallelism.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 自回归图像生成模型由于顺序逐 token 预测导致推理缓慢。本文提出并行雅可比解码 (PJD)，一种无需训练的解码方法，在二维空间域扩展草稿 token 以实现高效的空间并行精炼。PJD 调整注意力掩码并利用图像局部空间相关性，有效缓解误差传播，加速生成过程。该方法为视觉自回归模型的投机解码提供了新思路，但其直接应用目标为图像生成而非视觉语言模型。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 自回归图像生成模型由于顺序逐 token 预测导致推理缓慢。本文提出并行雅可比解码 (PJD)，一种无需训练的解码方法，在二维空间域扩展草稿 token 以实现高效的空间并行精炼。PJD 调整注意力掩码并利用图像局部空间相关性，有效缓解误差传播，加速生成过程。该方法为视觉自回归模型的投机解码提供了新思路，但其直接应用目标为图像生成而非视觉语言模型。

quick_skim.title: D^2SD: Accelerating Speculative Decoding with Dual Diffusion Draft Models
quick_skim.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates autoregressive large language model inference by drafting multiple tokens and verifying them in a single target-model forward pass. Recent diffusion-based drafters generate an entire block of tokens in parallel but usually commit to a single draft sequence per verification: once the first mismatch occurs, all subsequent draft tokens are discarded, resulting in a limited acceptance rate. Naively batching more draft candidate sequences only introduces a marginal improvement, as redundant or poorly placed branches increase the cost of drafting and verification without proportionally increasing the number of accepted tokens.   We propose D^2SD, a dual diffusion draft speculative decoding framework that organizes candidates into a confidence-guided prefix tree, where the first diffusion drafter generates a block along with per-position confidence scores that are used to identify the most likely rejection boundary and select the top-K prefix ranges for recovery; the second variable-prefix diffusion drafter re-anchors at each selected prefix and proposes alternative continuations in one batched pass; the resulting shared-prefix candidates are jointly verified via cascade attention. Empirically, D^2SD shows clear improvements over both the underlying diffusion approach and strong autoregressive speculative decoding baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: dual diffusion draft speculative decoding for LLM acceleration
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 双扩散草稿投机解码用于LLM加速
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 双扩散草稿投机解码用于LLM加速
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 双扩散草稿投机解码用于LLM加速
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes D^2SD, a dual diffusion draft framework that generates multiple candidate draft tree branches to improve acceptance rate in speculative decoding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对扩散草稿生成单序列导致接受率低的问题，提出双扩散草稿投机解码框架D^2SD，通过组织多个候选草稿分支树，在保持低开销的同时提升接受令牌数，从而加速LLM推理。实验表明该方法在多种模型和任务上优于现有扩散草稿方法。

quick_skim.title: Minimizing the Hidden Cost of Scales: Graph-Guided Ultra-Low-Bit Quantization for Large Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Post-training quantization (PTQ) is critical for the efficient deployment of large language models (LLMs). Recent ultra-low-bit PTQ methods rely on rigid weight-saliency assumptions or position heuristics, introducing substantial hidden scaling overhead. We propose SAGE-PTQ (Saliency-Aware Graph-guided Efficient PTQ), a novel ultra-low-bit quantization framework for LLMs that minimizes hidden scaling cost. SAGE-PTQ separates salient and unsalient weights using distributional statistics, then models subsampled unsalient weights as a sparse graph to estimate the optimal number of groups per layer. SAGE-PTQ applies dual-mode quantization, assigning multi-bit precision to salient weights and binarizing unsalient weights. To reduce scaling overhead, SAGE-PTQ uses one per-channel scale for salient weights and one scalar per unsalient group. Finally, SAGE-PTQ implements adaptive saliency thresholding to select the optimal saliency ratio per matrix. SAGE-PTQ achieves 1.03 weight bits and only 0.004 scaling bits per matrix on average, outperforming state-of-the-art methods such as BiLLM and PB-LLM. On LLaMA-3-8B, SAGE-PTQ achieves 6.74 WikiText2 perplexity, compared to 55.8 for BiLLM, while using less than 50% of BiLLM's GPU memory. On LLaMA-2-70B, SAGE-PTQ provides 1.5x faster decoding on one NVIDIA L40 GPU, demonstrating practical inference efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes ultra-low-bit post-training quantization for efficient LLM deployment
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出超低位后训练量化方法以实现大语言模型的高效部署
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出超低位后训练量化方法以实现大语言模型的高效部署
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出超低位后训练量化方法以实现大语言模型的高效部署
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SAGE-PTQ presents a graph-guided ultra-low-bit quantization framework for LLMs, separating salient and unsalient weights and using sparse graph modeling to minimize scaling overhead, enabling efficient deployment.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型后训练量化中的隐藏缩放开销问题，本文提出SAGE-PTQ，一种基于显著性感知图引导的超低位量化框架。该方法利用分布统计分离重要与不重要权重，对不重要权重建模为稀疏图以确定最优分组，对重要权重采用多比特精度，不重要权重二值化。实验表明，在保持模型质量的同时显著降低了内存和计算开销，为大语言模型高效推理提供了实用的量化方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对大语言模型后训练量化中的隐藏缩放开销问题，本文提出SAGE-PTQ，一种基于显著性感知图引导的超低位量化框架。该方法利用分布统计分离重要与不重要权重，对不重要权重建模为稀疏图以确定最优分组，对重要权重采用多比特精度，不重要权重二值化。实验表明，在保持模型质量的同时显著降低了内存和计算开销，为大语言模型高效推理提供了实用的量化方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 大语言模型高效推理相关的论文检索, especially work that connects or combines: efficient large language model inference techniques; KV cache optimization and management for LLM; speculative decoding for faster LLM inference; structured and unstructured pruning for LLMs; efficient attention mechanisms like FlashAttention or PagedAttention; survey on efficient inference of large language models; state of the art techniques for LLM inference acceleration; optimizing throughput and latency for LLM serving.
